# @mabulu-inc/schema-flow

> Declarative zero-downtime PostgreSQL migrations. Define the desired database state in YAML, and schema-flow figures out the diff.

Run via `npx @mabulu-inc/schema-flow` — zero install, no Java, no Go, no Docker.

## What It Does

Schema-flow reads YAML table definitions and compares them against a live PostgreSQL database. It generates and executes the minimal set of DDL statements to bring the database to the desired state. Pre- and post-migration SQL scripts handle procedural work like data backfills, grants, and seed data.

## Key Principles

- Safe by default: only additive operations unless `--allow-destructive` is passed
- Plan before apply: `plan` shows exactly what SQL would run without touching the database
- Convention over configuration: drop files in `schema/tables/`, `schema/pre/`, `schema/post/`
- SHA-256 hash tracking: only new or changed files are processed
- Advisory locking prevents concurrent migrations
- Lock timeout and statement timeout for safe DDL
- Retry logic for transient database errors

## Directory Structure

After `schema-flow init`, the project expects:

```
schema/
  tables/       YAML table definitions (one file per table)
  enums/        Enum type definitions
  functions/    Function definitions
  views/        View and materialized view definitions
  roles/        Role definitions
  pre/          Pre-migration SQL scripts (run before schema changes)
  post/         Post-migration SQL scripts (run after schema changes)
  mixins/       Reusable schema mixins (timestamps, soft_delete, etc.)
  repeatable/   SQL scripts re-run whenever their content changes
```

## CLI Commands

- `init` — Create the `schema/` directory structure
- `plan` — Dry run showing what would change
- `run` — Execute all phases: pre, migrate, post
- `run pre` — Run only pre-migration scripts
- `run migrate` — Run only declarative schema migration
- `run post` — Run only post-migration scripts
- `validate` — Validate schema against a live database (transaction, always rolls back)
- `drift` — Compare live database vs YAML and report differences
- `lint` — Static analysis of migration plan for dangerous patterns
- `sql` — Generate migration SQL file from plan
- `erd` — Generate Mermaid ERD diagram
- `down` — Show reverse migration plan (rollback)
- `down --apply` — Execute reverse migration
- `contract` — Drop columns previously marked for removal via expand/contract
- `expand-status` — Show pending expand/contract column renames
- `status` — Show migration history and current state
- `baseline` — Mark current database state as the baseline (skip existing objects)
- `generate` — Generate YAML from an existing database
- `generate --seeds` — Generate seed YAML from existing table data
- `new pre` / `new post` / `new mixin` — Scaffold new script files
- `docs` — Print YAML format reference

## Configuration

Database connection is resolved in order:
1. `--connection-string` CLI flag
2. `DATABASE_URL` environment variable
3. `schema-flow.config.yaml` in the project root

## Supported Schema Objects

- Tables and columns (all PostgreSQL types)
- Primary keys, unique constraints, check constraints
- Indexes (btree, gin, gist, hash, brin, partial, expression)
- Foreign keys (including cross-schema references)
- Enums (with safe value addition)
- Functions and procedures
- Views and materialized views
- RLS policies
- Roles and grants (GRANT on tables, schemas, functions)
- Triggers
- Generated columns (stored)
- Column and table comments
- Seeds (declarative row data)
- Mixins for DRY schema definitions

## YAML Table Definition

A table YAML file supports these top-level keys:

```yaml
table: table_name
use: [timestamps]             # optional, reference to schema/mixins/
columns:
  - name: id
    type: serial
    primary_key: true
  - name: email
    type: varchar(255)
    unique: true
    nullable: false            # default is false (NOT NULL)
    default: "'unknown'"
    references:
      table: other_table
      column: id
      schema: other_schema     # optional, for cross-schema FKs
      on_delete: CASCADE
      on_update: CASCADE
    comment: "User email address"
indexes:
  - columns: [email]
    unique: true
  - columns: ["lower(email)"]
    name: idx_users_email_lower
checks:
  - name: chk_email
    expression: "length(email) > 0"
rls: true
force_rls: true
policies:
  - name: user_isolation
    for: ALL
    using: "(user_id = current_setting('app.user_id')::int)"
triggers:
  - name: audit_trigger
    timing: AFTER
    events: [INSERT, UPDATE]
    function: audit_fn
    for_each: ROW
seeds:
  - id: 1
    email: "admin@example.com"
grants:
  - to: readonly_role
    privileges: [SELECT]
```

## Expand/Contract Pattern

For zero-downtime column renames:

```yaml
columns:
  - name: new_email
    type: varchar(255)
    expand:
      from: email
      transform: "lower(email)"
      reverse: "email"
```

Run `schema-flow run` to expand (add new column, dual-write trigger, backfill). Later run `schema-flow contract` to drop the old column.

## Detailed Documentation

- [README.md](README.md) — Full documentation with examples and all YAML options
- [docs/yaml-reference.md](docs/yaml-reference.md) — Complete YAML schema specification
- [docs/switching-from-imperative.md](docs/switching-from-imperative.md) — Migrating from Knex, Flyway, Liquibase, dbmate
- [docs/switching-from-orms.md](docs/switching-from-orms.md) — Migrating from Prisma, TypeORM, Sequelize, Django, Rails
- [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) — Development setup and contribution guidelines
- [examples/](examples/) — Example projects (todo-app, CMS, multi-schema, multi-tenant)

## Source Code

- [src/cli/](src/cli/) — CLI command handlers
- [src/planner/](src/planner/) — Migration plan generation (diff engine)
- [src/introspect/](src/introspect/) — Live database introspection
- [src/executor/](src/executor/) — SQL execution with advisory locking
- [src/schema/](src/schema/) — YAML parsing and validation
- [src/drift/](src/drift/) — Drift detection (YAML vs live DB)
- [src/rollback/](src/rollback/) — Reverse migration generation
- [src/erd/](src/erd/) — Mermaid ERD diagram generation
- [src/expand/](src/expand/) — Expand/contract column rename logic
- [src/scaffold/](src/scaffold/) — `init` and `new` command scaffolding
- [src/lint/](src/lint/) — Static analysis rules
- [src/sql/](src/sql/) — SQL file generation
